attacking_style: Attacks after climb summits or in final 2-5km, exploiting hesitation and using time-trial power to solo rather than pure climbing accelerations. Prefers descents and post-summit transitions over steep gradients.
team_tactics: Quick-Step prioritizes him for stage hunting over GC support. Often works as dual-threat domestique before attacking. Team allows him to animate races but he sometimes refuses chase duty to protect energy.
terrain_preference: Medium-mountain stages with rolling finales, medium-length time trials, and cobbled classics where positioning allows. Post-summit descents and transitions suit diesel engine better than steep explosive climbs.
vulnerability: Exposed on pure climbing accelerations by GC specialists (shed by AG2R tempo on climbs). Pacing errors under pressure as pre-race favorite. Form inconsistency leads to multi-year droughts between peaks.
key_patterns: Attacks post-summit rather than on climbs, exploiting favorites' hesitation (Liege 2018, Tour 2022)
Time-trials final kilometers solo after group fragmentation, using diesel power (Giro 2017 x2)
Shows dual-threat capacity: domestique work at 3km then attacks at 800m (Catalunya 2018)
Late solo moves from 11-55km out, gambling on peloton miscalculating chase (Tour 2020, Fleche 2017)
Strong time-trial capability rewarding medium-mountain courses despite non-specialist status (Giro 2017 TT)
Form volatility: multi-year drought broken 2022 but disappointed as favorite 2019 Paris-Nice TT
form_trajectory: positive: 2022 Tour breakthrough after multi-year drought showed form transformation, winning 55km solo after sprinting previous day, suggesting career renaissance after 2018-2020 struggles
